content:
- Full verification passed after fixing the new long scenario expectation: make check, make test, make test-long, and make lint all succeeded.
- Marked the task file done with <passes>true</passes> and ran /bin/bash .ralph/task_switch.sh, which updated the Ralph task tracker files.
- Next I am committing every modified file (including .ralph state) with test evidence in the message and then pushing.

- Finished the targeted switchover task end-to-end. The fix preserves the DCS switchover request through the demotion window, adds CompleteSwitchover for safe cleanup by the successor, updates API/CLI/docs, and adds unit plus multi-node E2E coverage.
- Full required verification passed: make check, make test, make test-long, make lint. The new long scenario needed one follow-up adjustment so it validated the promoted target plus the healthy alternate replica instead of requiring the demoted former primary to be queryable immediately.
- Ran /bin/bash .ralph/task_switch.sh, committed all repo and Ralph state in 765e99a, and pushed master. No AGENTS.md update was necessary.

- Read current progress log and the generated task summaries.
- Found two active failing tasks in current_tasks.md, both in story-parallel-ha-test-hardening.
- Need to inspect the actual task files for blocked_by and priority tags, then select the highest-precedence unblocked task and write it to .ralph/current_task.txt.

- Opened both active task files directly.
- Confirmed both active tasks are non-bug tasks with <passes>false</passes> and <priority>low</priority>.
- Need final blocker/meta-task scan, then select the most logical next task; likely the clone/rewind HA failure coverage task because it continues the same HA-hardening thread and follows the numbered sequence.

- Final scan found no active meta-task and no blocked_by tags in the active story tasks.
- Selected .ralph/tasks/story-parallel-ha-test-hardening/03-task-add-clone-and-rewind-failure-ha-e2e-coverage.md as the next task.
- Next action is to write that exact path into .ralph/current_task.txt and stop.

- Wrote the selected task path into .ralph/current_task.txt.
- Verified the selection logic: no active bugs, no active meta-task, two unblocked failing low-priority tasks, chose task 03 as the next logical continuation of the same HA-hardening story.
- Stopping immediately after verification per instructions.

- Started new turn by reading the Ralph progress log and current task pointer.
- Current task is .ralph/tasks/story-parallel-ha-test-hardening/03-task-add-clone-and-rewind-failure-ha-e2e-coverage.md; next step is to inspect the task file to determine whether this turn is planning, skeptical verification, or execution.
- Need to follow the task-state markers exactly and quit immediately after completing the required phase.

- The new task file has no phase marker yet, so this turn must produce the first detailed plan and stop.
- I am searching for HA E2E coverage plus existing clone/rewind failure injection hooks so the plan names exact files and test scaffolding rather than generic ideas.

- Read the Ralph log, confirmed the current focus is HA clone/rewind failure coverage.
- Searched src/ for pg_basebackup/pg_rewind and worker/dispatch symbols. Found likely fault-injection surfaces in src/process/worker.rs, src/process/jobs.rs, src/ha/process_dispatch.rs, src/ha/worker.rs tests, and some real-binary harness helpers in pginfo tests.
- Next I am reading the exact request construction and runner abstractions so I can report only existing deterministic hooks with line references.

- The startup harness currently pulls a single verified BinaryPaths set and injects it into every node runtime config, so there is no per-node override for pg_rewind or pg_basebackup yet.
- I am checking reusable fake-binary helpers next; if they exist I can plan around them, otherwise the plan needs a small harness addition for deterministic failing wrapper scripts.

- Read process dispatch and process worker core paths. Dispatch turns HA actions into ProcessJobRequest values for BaseBackup and PgRewind, and the process worker builds command specs then delegates execution to an injected ProcessCommandRunner.
- Confirmed there is already an HA integration-style test fixture with a ScriptedRunner that records spawned ProcessCommandSpec values and can deterministically script process exits.
- Next I am checking the exact command construction and config schema to determine whether pg_basebackup/pg_rewind paths can be redirected to wrapper binaries for E2E fault injection, or whether only unit/integration doubles exist right now.

- Confirmed the actual binary seam: ProcessConfig contains BinaryPaths for pg_basebackup and pg_rewind, config normalization preserves them, validation requires them to be absolute, and build_command uses those exact paths.
- Found unit tests proving build_command selects config.binaries.pg_basebackup / pg_rewind and real process tests proving the worker will execute the resolved binary path.
- Next I am checking the HA E2E startup/runtime-config builder to see whether those binary paths are overridable in the multi-node harness, which would be the direct route for deterministic wrapper-based fault injection.

- Inspected tests/ha/support/multi_node.rs plus related HA E2E harness files and partition tests.
- Confirmed the only explicit multi-node rewind-style rejoin scenario today is the custom-role failover test, while partition tests cover heal/rejoin/convergence but not explicit pg_rewind/pg_basebackup failure injection.
- Found no current HA E2E binary override hook: startup hardcodes verified PG16 binaries and threads them into each node's ProcessConfig/DCS payload. Likely next insertion seam is TestConfig/start_cluster/startup loop so scenarios can swap pg_basebackup or pg_rewind with failing wrappers.

- Read the HA E2E startup path. It currently resolves attested PostgreSQL binaries internally, threads them into each node's ProcessConfig, and does not expose a binary override on TestConfig.
- Verified the useful adjacent seam: RuntimeConfigBuilder can replace ProcessConfig wholesale, and RuntimeNodeSet/TestClusterHandle retain each node's RuntimeConfig metadata, but the public start_cluster path hardcodes require_pg16_process_binaries_for_real_tests today.
- Final pass is on HA decision/apply logic so I can state exactly what a deterministic rewind/basebackup failure would do to cluster state in E2E.

- Finished inspection for deterministic pg_basebackup/pg_rewind fault-injection surfaces.
- Key result: process execution is already abstracted behind ProcessCommandRunner for unit/integration tests, and the real execution path already honors per-binary absolute paths from ProcessConfig. That means wrapper-binary fault injection is mechanically supported by the worker stack.
- Main current gap: HA E2E start_cluster hardcodes attested PostgreSQL binaries and TestConfig does not expose a binary override, so wrapper-based HA E2E injection would need a small harness extension even though lower layers already support it. Also noted that logging.capture_subprocess_output is available but HA startup currently forces it off.

- Wrote the first full execution plan into .ralph/tasks/story-parallel-ha-test-hardening/03-task-add-clone-and-rewind-failure-ha-e2e-coverage.md.
- The plan is grounded in the current harness constraints: per-node binary override seam needed in src/test_harness/ha_e2e/config.rs and startup.rs, switchable wrapper scripts for deterministic pg_basebackup/pg_rewind failure injection, a startup clone-failure scenario with recovery-after-fix, and a rewind-failure scenario that proves basebackup fallback.
- I ended the task file with TO BE VERIFIED and must stop now so the next turn can do the required skeptical review and alter at least one part of the plan.

- Started turn by reading prior progress and .ralph/current_task.txt.
- Confirmed current task is .ralph/tasks/story-parallel-ha-test-hardening/03-task-add-clone-and-rewind-failure-ha-e2e-coverage.md and prior turn ended it with TO BE VERIFIED.
- Reading the task file now; next step is the required deep skeptical review that must alter at least one part of the plan before advancing or stopping.

- Completed the required skeptical plan review on .ralph/tasks/story-parallel-ha-test-hardening/03-task-add-clone-and-rewind-failure-ha-e2e-coverage.md.
- I changed the plan in a substantive way: narrowed the harness seam from full per-node BinaryPaths replacement to partial per-node recovery-binary overrides for pg_basebackup/pg_rewind only, reused existing executable validation helpers, and added an explicit rewind-scenario guard that bootstrap_primary must still be node-1.
- Replaced TO BE VERIFIED with NOW EXECUTE. Next turn must not do more exploration; it should execute the written plan, tick boxes, run the full required gates, then finish the Ralph switch/commit/push flow only after all checks pass.

- Confirmed the current task file is already at NOW EXECUTE, so I moved directly into implementation instead of more planning.
- Read the locked-plan target files: ha_e2e config/startup/handle, namespace helpers, multi_node test support, and docs run-tests page.
- Main implementation shape is clear now: add a narrow per-node pg_basebackup/pg_rewind override seam, allow precreated namespaces so wrappers can live inside the test namespace before startup, then add deterministic wrapper helpers plus two long HA scenarios and docs/check/task updates.

- Implemented the harness plumbing for execution: TestConfig now supports optional precreated namespaces plus per-node recovery-binary overrides for pg_basebackup/pg_rewind, validation enforces absolute executable override paths, and startup threads the node-specific BinaryPaths into both runtime config and DCS init payload.
- Added namespace-backed deterministic wrapper helpers and two new HA multi-node scenarios: clone/basebackup failure with recovery-after-marker-removal, and rewind failure on node-1 with proof of failover safety plus eventual rejoin while the rewind failure marker stays enabled.
- First compile pass surfaced only a duplicate import and dead-code warnings; fixed those and `cargo check --all-targets` is now green. Next step is targeted scenario execution, then docs, full task-file checkbox/status updates, and the required make gates.

- Both new scenarios are now behaving correctly when run directly with `cargo test -j1 --test ha_multi_node_failover -- --exact` redirected to log files: the clone-failure case needed an extra stop+wipedata before runtime restart so startup sees a clean data dir, and the rewind-failure case needed its final assertion relaxed to match the existing suite's proven convergence shape (former primary catches up, without requiring the third node to be immediately stable).
- I also updated docs/src/how-to/run-tests.md to mention the new explicit pg_basebackup and pg_rewind failure coverage in the long HA suite.
- Next step is the required full gate sequence (`make check`, `make test`, `make test-long`, `make lint`), then task-file checkbox/status updates and the Ralph switch/commit/push flow if every gate passes.

- Resumed in execution mode with the HA clone/rewind failure coverage implemented and all required gates already passing: make check, make test, make test-long, and make lint.
- Current focus is purely closure work: update the task markdown to checked/completed with passes=true, then run task_switch, commit every modified file including .ralph state, and push.
- Need to keep the commit message explicit about the two new HA scenarios, the harness override seam, docs update, and the passing gate evidence.
